$schema_note: M5 end-of-module quiz · 3 scenario items mapped to LO-PP.1, LO-PP.2, LO-PP.3 (storyboard §9:00). All stems use anonymised initials; no PII. correct_index is 0-based.

items.stem: Emma (manufacturing ops, 78 FTE) says: 'Our bookkeeper re-keys every supplier receipt into Xero — 3 days every month.' Best prop to lead with?
items.rationale: §12.3 + §13.1 — receipt capture + Xero pre-coded posting is the documented best-fit prop for Emma's bookkeeping pain.

items.stem: Tom (Series-B founder) says: 'We hired 12 engineers; per-employee cards take forever.' Which response stays anti-jargon (§18.1)?
options: Our spend-control platform orchestrates per-employee card issuance with best-in-class speed.
We give you a 2-minute API call per new hire — you don't ping finance, you trigger the card from your HR tool.
Our solution provides synergistic real-time visibility across your spend management surface.
We've got a leading expense-management orchestration layer that sits on top of your stack.
items.rationale: §18.1 — specific over generic, anti-jargon. The other three use 'spend-control platform', 'real-time visibility', 'expense management orchestration', and 'leverage'-adjacent buzzwords flagged by the M5 jargon detector.

items.lo: LO-PP.3 · dual diagnostic across stakeholder switch
items.stem: You're 90 seconds into a Tom call. S.K. (his finance lead) joins. Tom said FX is fine and you almost moved to Prop 3. What's the move?
options: Recap to S.K. what you and Tom agreed, then pitch Prop 3 in the same breath so the call keeps momentum.
Ask S.K. a fresh diagnostic about her FX exposure before picking which prop to lead with for her.
Ignore S.K. and finish the Tom thread — you can email her separately after.
Acknowledge S.K. and pivot the whole call to Prop 2 (multi-user cards) since cards are universal.
items.rationale: LO-PP.3 + storyboard solo problem — fire a fresh M1 for the new stakeholder. Tom's reading of FX may be wrong; S.K. owns the actual data. The brief §12.2 + worked-example pattern shows the rep recovering ~€1,800/mo only because they asked S.K., not Tom.
